question.text: Market sizing using a top-down approach involves:

answer.text: Aggregating revenue of all individual companies in the industry
Starting from macro-economic factors and narrowing down to industry level

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Top-down market sizing starts from macro-economic factors and arrives at the industry level. Bottom-up aggregates individual company data. **The Trap:** Aggregating company revenues is the bottom-up approach. Company financials and surveys are data inputs, not the sizing methodology. **Cross-Exam Context:** Market sizing approaches tested in NISM Series XV Chapter 6. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Both approaches have pros and cons. Top-down may overestimate (not all demand is captured); bottom-up may underestimate (missing unorganized players).
